text: I feel a bit swept up in the lively energy around me. There’s chaos, movement, and laughter, and I sense an eagerness to capture and remember this moment—a desire to document, perhaps out of pride or camaraderie. Yet, there’s also a tug of regret about time slipping away and not being able to fully participate in both living and recording. Amid the swirl of bodies and voices, I am present—still, slightly detached, yet warmly included in the unfolding memory.
